query: Iran IAEA cooperation law suspension 2025 2026, current state of Iran nuclear inspections and diplomacy, US Iran nuclear deal negotiations status June July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Iran-IAEA cooperation and US-Iran nuclear diplomacy as of July 2026:

**IAEA Cooperation Law — Still Suspended**: Iran's July 2, 2025 law suspending cooperation with the IAEA (passed 221-1) remains in effect. The IAEA halted all verification activities in Iran after February 28, 2026, and cannot verify Iran's uranium stockpile. Iran has denied access to 20 declared sites.

**The Wars Reshaped Everything**: Two conflicts (June 2025 "12-day war" and the larger 2026 Iran War beginning Feb 28, 2026, which included the assassination of Khamenei) destroyed ~22,000 centrifuges. Iran's last-verified stockpile (June 2025) held 440.9 kg of 60% HEU, now of unknown whereabouts.

**Islamabad Memorandum (June 17, 2026)**: A 14-point MoU ended the war. Iran agreed to down-blend enriched uranium under IAEA supervision and never build nuclear weapons, with a 60-day (extendable) window for a final deal and US sanctions/oil waivers through Aug 21, 2026.

**Current Diplomacy**: Technical talks continued in Doha (June 30–July 1, 2026) with "positive progress," mediated by Qatar and Pakistan. Key disputes remain: IAEA access timing, enrichment suspension duration (US ~15 years vs. Iran shorter), whether enriched material is shipped abroad, $6bn frozen assets, and Israel's refusal to be bound. The ceasefire remains fragile with intermittent strikes into early July.

Full structured findings, data points, and sources have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Iran's Suspension of IAEA Cooperation is a Political Gamble, not a Technical Decision
sources.snippet: On June 25, Iran's parliament approved a bill 'Suspension of Cooperation with the IAEA' with 221 votes in favor and a lone abstention; swiftly ratified by the Guardian Council.

sources.title: Iran president signs law suspending cooperation with IAEA
sources.snippet: President Pezeshkian signed a law suspending cooperation with the IAEA amid growing Tehran-IAEA tensions.

sources.title: Analysis of IAEA Iran Verification and Monitoring and NPT Safeguards Reports — June 2026
sources.snippet: IAEA cannot provide any information on the current stockpile; ~22,000 centrifuges destroyed; verification halted after Feb 28, 2026; 20 sites access denied.

sources.title: 2025-2026 Iran-United States negotiations
sources.snippet: Chronology of negotiations, 2026 war, and Islamabad Memorandum signed June 17, 2026.

sources.title: Fighting Mars U.S.-Iranian Pledges to End War, Restart Nuclear Talks
sources.snippet: 14-point MoU terms, down-blending under IAEA supervision, 60-day deadline, oil waiver through Aug 21 2026, disputes over enrichment duration and material location.

sources.title: Iran to open 'communication channel' on MoU with US after talks in Qatar
sources.snippet: Doha talks June 30-July 1, 2026; $6bn frozen assets; communication channel to report MoU breaches; partial Hormuz resumption.

sources.title: UN nuclear chief says Iran inspections will happen, Tehran says after deal
sources.snippet: Grossi says MoU explicitly requires IAEA supervision; Iran says access resolved only within final agreement and conditioned on sanctions termination.

sources.title: Analysis: The Iran MOU is a mess. It still might work
sources.snippet: Brett McGurk: the 14-point two-page MoU is vague, grants up-front US concessions for reopening the already-open Strait of Hormuz.

sources.title: Iran War 2026 -- Day 130 Update -- 07 July 2026
sources.snippet: No new US strikes reported in 6-7 July window; July 7 projectile attack on tanker in Strait of Hormuz; stand-down agreed June 29 held.

sources.title: IAEA chief says Iran inspections deal 'possible but terribly difficult' after 12-day war
sources.snippet: Grossi (Feb 13, 2026): new inspections agreement 'absolutely possible' but 'terribly difficult' after dramatic shift in Iran's nuclear landscape.

key_facts: Iran's parliament passed the 'Suspension of Cooperation with the IAEA' law on June 25, 2025 (221 votes in favor, 1 abstention); President Pezeshkian signed it into law July 2, 2025. It remains in effect as of July 2026.

The IAEA stopped all verification activities in Iran after February 28, 2026 (start of the 2026 war); inspectors had been withdrawn by end of June 2025. Iran has denied access to 20 declared nuclear sites.
On June 12, 2025, the IAEA declared Iran in breach of its NPT non-proliferation obligations (first such finding in ~20 years). Iran terminated the Cairo Agreement on Nov 20, 2025.
The IAEA cannot currently verify the size, composition, or whereabouts of Iran's enriched uranium stockpile.
Last verified stockpile (June 13, 2025): ~9,040 kg UF6 plus 834 kg other forms, including 440.9 kg at 60% enrichment, 184.1 kg at 20%, and 6,024.4 kg at 5%.
Military strikes destroyed ~22,000 gas centrifuges; per ISIS, Iran for the first time in 20 years has no identifiable route to produce weapon-grade uranium via centrifuge enrichment.
The US and Iran signed the 14-point Islamabad Memorandum (MoU) on June 17, 2026, ending the 2026 war; Trump signed at Versailles after G7, Pezeshkian in Tehran.

MoU nuclear terms: Iran reaffirmed it will never develop nuclear weapons and agreed to down-blend enriched uranium under IAEA supervision; enrichment levels deferred to a final deal with a 60-day (extendable) negotiation deadline.
US committed to terminate all sanctions (primary, secondary, UN); Treasury issued an oil-sales waiver through August 21, 2026. ~$6 billion in frozen Iranian assets to partly fund purchases of goods.

Second round of technical talks held in Doha, Qatar June 30-July 1, 2026 (Qatar/Pakistan mediated); working groups on nuclear, sanctions, monitoring, and dispute resolution reported 'positive progress.'

IAEA chief Grossi says inspections 'are going to happen' and timing is 'not essential'; Iran's Deputy FM Gharibabadi says access to attacked sites will only be resolved within a final agreement and conditioned on sanctions termination.

Key unresolved disputes: enrichment suspension duration (US ~15 years vs Iran shorter), whether enriched material is shipped abroad or retained domestically, and Israel's refusal to be bound by the interim deal.

Ceasefire is fragile: it briefly collapsed mid-June 2026 amid alleged Iranian drone attacks on shipping; intermittent strikes continued, with a July 7, 2026 projectile attack on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz reported. Strait traffic only partially resumed.
The 2026 war (began Feb 28, 2026) included the assassinations of Supreme Leader Khamenei and negotiator Ali Larijani; a temporary ceasefire was announced April 7, 2026.

data_points.metric: Iran parliament vote to suspend IAEA cooperation (votes in favor)
Iran enriched uranium at 60% (last IAEA-verified, pre-strike)
Total enriched uranium in UF6 form (last IAEA-verified)
Gas centrifuges destroyed by military strikes
Iran declared nuclear sites denied IAEA access
Frozen Iranian assets referenced in talks
